creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text: If a fuse filesystem sets FOPEN_KEEP_CACHE in the response to FUSE_OPEN, then the kernel should not flush its read caches on subsequent open(2)s of the same file.  Our fuse(4) driver does indeed check for that flag in fuse_vnode_open.  However, the check doesn't work, because the flag isn't saved from the previous FUSE_OPEN.  The flag is guaranteed to be unset in that code path.  The solution would be to save the open_flags in the vnode.
